verses.text: 'The fool hath said in his heart, There is no God,' and corruption follows his denial. This truth is repeated from Psalm 14 for emphasis. The folly of denying God is so grave it bears saying twice.
God looks down from heaven and finds all gone aside, 'there is none that doeth good, no, not one.' The whole race is fallen. Apart from grace, none seeks God or does true good.
God looks down from heaven and finds all gone aside, 'there is none that doeth good, no, not one.' The whole race is fallen. Apart from grace, none seeks God or does true good.

The workers of iniquity, who devour God's people, are seized with sudden, groundless fear, for God scatters them. Guilt breeds terror. The wicked tremble where no real cause exists, while God stands with the righteous.

The workers of iniquity, who devour God's people, are seized with sudden, groundless fear, for God scatters them. Guilt breeds terror. The wicked tremble where no real cause exists, while God stands with the righteous.
'Oh that the salvation of Israel were come out of Zion!' David longs for deliverance. That cry is answered in Christ, the salvation God has sent forth from Zion.
